steps.title: Find the middle positions
steps.content:
The frequency table has a total of
$$
2+3+4+2+1=12
$$
data values. Since there is an even number of values, the median is the average of the values in positions $6$ and $7$.

steps.title: Use cumulative frequency
steps.content: The first $2$ values are $11$, the next $3$ values are $12$, and the next $4$ values are $13$. So positions $6$ and $7$ are both $13$.

steps.title: Compute the median
steps.content: Average the two middle values: $$ \frac{13+13}{2}=13 $$ The answer is C.
